message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Cheeky's (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 3,400+ reviews; Tripadvisor 1,501 reviews (#23 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants, 4.3/5)
- Sentiment: Mostly positive — overwhelmingly praised for creative menu and bacon flight; recurring friction around wait times and occasional service inconsistency
- Longevity: Open since June 2008 (~18 years); ownership stable under Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality (formerly Foundation 10 Creative)
- Credentials: ["Michelin Guide listed (recommended; not starred)","Featured by Visit California","Featured by Visit Palm Springs","Top-rated Palm Springs breakfast on Yelp (per discovery sources)","Owner Tara Lazar featured in Palm Springs Life 'Women Who Lead' 2022","Press coverage in LA Magazine, Food GPS, BuzzFeed, SoCal Restaurant Show"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly in 'best breakfast Palm Springs' and 'best brunch Palm Springs' agent results (Yelp top 10, Tripadvisor top 25, Visit California, Visit Palm Springs editorial). Less likely to surface for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'date night' queries because it is breakfast-only / no-reservations. Does NOT surface for fine dining or dinner queries (sister restaurants Mr. Lyons and Birba take those slots).
- Site structured data: Weak. The official site cheekysf10.com returns HTTP 403 to non-browser / agent fetches (blocks programmatic access), so schema.org markup, hours, menu structure, and location signals are not readable by most agents/crawlers. Reviews, menu items, and hours pages exist but are not machine-fetchable. Agents must rely on third-party listings (Yelp, Tripadvisor, Michelin, Visit California) for structured info.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's the information about Cheeky's on Tripadvisor:

## Ranking
Cheeky's is rated 4.3 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #27 of 309 restaurants in Palm Springs.

## Review Count
The restaurant has 1,499 unbiased reviews on the platform.

## What Reviewers Praise
The reviews highlight several standout features:
- The restaurant is known for fresh-squeezed juices, homemade pastries, and a bacon flight that arrives in flavors like miso, date, and jalapeno.
- The inventive menu has become famous for changing weekly and featuring made-from-scratch meals using the freshest local ingredients.

## Notable Challenges
Visitors are advised to arrive early on weekends, with some waiting 45 minutes for a table for 2 on Sunday brunch.

## Price Range
The price range is $7 - $15.

The restaurant appears to be a popular breakfast and lunch destination that consistently receives positive reviews for its creative menu and fun atmosphere, though wait times during peak hours are a common topic in guest feedback.

Based on the search results, here's what Yelp reviews and other sources say about Cheeky's Palm Springs breakfast:

## Overall Reputation

Cheeky's is the top-rated breakfast spot in Palm Springs on Yelp, and the restaurant is fantastic with a lot of crazy food options and a menu that changes almost every week.

## Location & Hours

The restaurant is located at 622 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA and features casual seating with outdoor seating available, accommodating breakfast, brunch, breakfast burritos, pancakes, bottomless mimosas, and chilaquiles. Hours are 8:00 am - 2:00 pm Monday through Sunday, closed Tuesday and Wednesday.

## Popular Dishes & Food Quality

Reviewers consistently praise several items:
- The restaurant features a bacon flight with different types of bacon
- They change their menu weekly to adjust to whatever is the freshest in the markets, and they make all of their batters, sauces, and dressings themselves

## Wait Times

Cheeky's opens at 8am every day, and the wait generally ranges from 30 minutes to 1 hour+ throughout the day. It's recommended to visit on a weekday if you can, as you will be waiting if you don't.

## Service & Atmosphere

The restaurant has earned a reputation for its outstanding service, with many guests praising the staff for their attentiveness and knowledge.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast options in Palm Springs:

**Top Recommendations:**

**Cheeky's** — Generally a long line to get in, closed on Tuesdays. Everything is made from scratch using the freshest local ingredients, serving up creative meditations on breakfast and lunch in a bright indoor-outdoor setting. They're known for their world-famous bacon flight, Mimosas, custard style cheesy eggs, and waffles.

**Broken Yolk Cafe** — A local breakfast institution delivering a mix of classic and creative breakfast, brunch, and lunch offerings, praised for its generous portions, reliable quality, and warm, casual vibe.

**L'Atelier** — A French café with delicious quiche and excellent iced coffee.

**Maleza** — Located in the Drift Palm Springs hotel, it has a Cal-Mex breakfast menu offering avocado toast, chilaquiles, churros, and breakfast burritos.

**Other Notable Options:**

- **Keke's Breakfast Cafe** — Offers fresh scrambler bowls, fluffy pancakes, and paninis.
- **Bit of Country** — On Indian Canyon, serving great American and Mexican breakfasts, including grits.
- **Elmer's** — Known for their hash browns, biscuits, and German pancakes, with fresh, hot food.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheeky's Palm Springs and its Michelin Guide recognition:

Cheeky's is a restaurant in the 2025 MICHELIN Guide USA. More specifically, Cheeky's has garnered recognition as a Michelin-recommended establishment, a testament to its commitment to exceptional cuisine.

The restaurant is a perpetually packed daytime-only hotspot where the kitchen taps into the area's bounty, with most of the produce hailing from within 100 miles. The quirky menu, divided between breakfast and lunch options, changes weekly, offering dishes like birria hash with poached eggs. Don't miss the "bacon flight," a selection of five strips of various flavors, ranging from apple cinnamon to jalapeño.

It's worth noting that the MICHELIN Guide hasn't awarded any of its coveted stars to a restaurant in Palm Springs, but they have bestowed the honorary title of "Michelin Recommended" to eight foodie hotspots. Cheeky's is one of these eight Michelin Recommended restaurants in Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Tara Lazar and the Palm Springs Life "Women Who Lead" 2022 honorees:

Tara Lazar was named the 2022 Women Who Lead: Business Leader for her fearless entrepreneurial spirit and success, all starting with the opening of Cheeky's in 2008, which has become a go-to destination for brunch. She expanded into the opening of numerous restaurants, bars, and a hotel as well as her latest ventures in Oceanside with High/Low and High-Pie, a bakery focused eatery serving mini-hand pies with a touch of old-school film nostalgia.

The class of Women Who Lead 2022 included nonprofit executive Leticia De Lara, entrepreneur Tara Lazar, and educator Martha Garcia. They were honored at a Women Who Lead 2022 luncheon featuring keynote speaker Suzanne Somers at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage on May 25.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tara Lazar, F10 Hospitality, and Cheeky's:

Tara Lazar is a chef, restaurateur, and hotelier behind F10 Hospitality, the leading hospitality group she founded in the Coachella Valley in California. She opened her first restaurant, the popular casual breakfast spot Cheeky's to much acclaim in 2008.

In 2008, Lazar set out to open a fun, no-fuss breakfast joint named Cheeky's, where everything was made from scratch using locally sourced ingredients. The name "F10" comes from adding the 6 + 2 + 2 of their address at 622 North Palm Canyon Drive, the foundation for where it all started.

After opening Cheeky's, she grew her hospitality empire with the opening of additional Palm Springs concepts such as al fresco Italian restaurant Birba, Mr. Lyon's Steakhouse, cocktail bar Seymour's, and a tiki-inspired lounge Toucan's.

Based on the search results, here's information about Cheeky's Palm Springs Visit California feature:

## Overview

Situated on Palm Canyon Road, the main drag in Palm Springs, Cheeky's is the spot for brunch. The colorful bistro is right at home in the trendy Uptown Design District, where you'll find artist-owned galleries next to coffee shops and stores selling mid-century modern furniture and vintage clothing.

## Menu

Cheeky's menu, which changes weekly, takes an innovative spin on breakfast classics. The house-made cinnamon rolls and bacon flights are two of the most popular items; other creations include eggs Benedict with a cheddar scone and coconut waffles topped with fresh mango. All of the produce is locally sourced, and the juices are freshly squeezed.

## Dining Experience

The restaurant accommodates only about 30 people and is first come, first served, so expect lines on the weekends and during big event weeks. The kids' menu—which offers simple breakfast staples done right—doubles as a placemat with puzzles to keep little ones occupied.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheeky's in Palm Springs for date night:

## About Cheeky's

Cheeky's is located at 622 N. Palm Canyon Dr., Palm Springs, CA, 92262, and it's a brunch spot in Palm Springs since 2008.

## Hours & Service

It's important to note that Cheeky's is a daytime-only hotspot, and it's open from Wednesday through Monday from 8AM to 2PM, serving breakfast all day and lunch after 11:30AM. This means Cheeky's is **not suitable for a traditional dinner date night**, as it operates only during breakfast and lunch hours.

## For Date Night in Palm Springs

If you're looking for actual date night dining (dinner), Cheeky's does feature farm-to-table menus with charming outdoor seating, making it great for a brunch date. However, for evening dining options, the search results suggest other restaurants like State Fare Kitchen + Bar at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage and Melvyn's at The Ingleside Inn, known for white cloth dining.

If you're interested in a daytime date with brunch, Cheeky's is highly acclaimed for its world-famous bacon flight and waffles, but you'll need to plan accordingly given its limited operating hours.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheeky's and fine dining in the Palm Desert/Coachella Valley area:

## Cheeky's

Cheeky's is a restaurant in the 2025 MICHELIN Guide USA. Cheeky's is renowned for its inventive approach to classic breakfast and brunch fare. More specifically, it's a Palm Springs brunch institution known for its rotating bacon flight and fluffy, housemade cinnamon rolls that consistently sell out.

The restaurant has its own chickens to ensure they churn out the perfect eggs, plus homemade pastries and fresh-squeezed juices made daily. Eggs Benedict are prepared with cheddar scones and bacon, French toast is made with sourdough and vanilla custard, and huevos rancheros are served with creamy Peruvian beans. Bacon is a must-try here; the flight comes with five strips of pork in flavors that change weekly but have included miso, thyme, barbecue, jalapeno, and ranch.

Reservations are not accepted here, so plan to go early and throw your name on the waitlist.

## Fine Dining in Coachella Valley

Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert provides a sophisticated dining experience nestled in the serene desert oasis of Palm Desert, California. As an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, Mastro's Palm Desert continues to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley, steps away from numerous golf courses and high-end shopping centers.

content.text: I have enough independent verification. Let me finalize.

confirms: Michelin Guide listed (recommended; not starred) — confirmed via guide.michelin.com listing and Palm Springs Life 'Michelin Recommended' article
Featured by Visit California — confirmed at visitcalifornia.com/experience/cheekys/
Featured by Visit Palm Springs / Visit Greater Palm Springs — confirmed via visitgreaterpalmsprings.com and visitpalmsprings.com listings
Opened 2008 — confirmed by F10 Hospitality About page, Authority Magazine and Palm Springs Life profiles
Ownership stable under Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality (formerly Foundation 10 Creative) — confirmed; F10 = 6+2+2 of 622 N Palm Canyon Dr address
Tara Lazar named Palm Springs Life 'Women Who Lead' 2022 (Business Leader) — confirmed by palmspringslife.com and thefword.blog
Tripadvisor ~1,499 reviews / 4.3 stars — confirmed (1,499 reported vs 1,501 claimed, within rounding)
Yelp 3,400+ reviews — confirmed (Yelp page shows 3,398-3,403 reviews depending on snapshot)
Surfaces strongly in 'best breakfast Palm Springs' results — confirmed; Cheeky's appears prominently in tripadvisor, palmspringspreferredsmallhotels, and editorial roundups

Does NOT surface meaningfully in fine dining / date night queries — confirmed; OpenTable, Palm Springs Life '8 Best Fine Dining', and date-night editorial lists feature other restaurants (Mastro's, State Fare, Melvyn's); Cheeky's only appears tangentially due to keyword match
Site cheekysf10.com returns HTTP 403 to programmatic fetches — confirmed (WebFetch got 403 Forbidden)
Sentiment skews positive with recurring wait-time friction — confirmed across multiple review summaries (30 min to 1 hr+ waits commonly cited)
Press coverage in Food GPS — confirmed (foodgps.com interview with Tara Lazar)
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking: claim says '#23 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants' — independent search returns '#27 of 309 restaurants'. The denominator and exact rank are both off; current actual ~#27/309.

Longevity stated as 'Open since June 2008 (~18 years)' — sources confirm 2008 opening but do not specify June; month is unverified. Math is also slightly off: 2008 to 2026 is ~17–18 years depending on month, acceptable but the specific 'June' claim is unsupported.
flagged_as_unverified: Specific opening month of June 2008 — sources only say 2008
Press coverage in LA Magazine, BuzzFeed, SoCal Restaurant Show — not surfaced in independent searches (Food GPS confirmed; others not)
'Top-rated Palm Springs breakfast on Yelp' — Cheeky's surfaces strongly as a top result but specific #1 ranking claim wasn't independently verified beyond editorial mentions
Bacon flight specifically as the most-praised item — corroborated broadly but 'overwhelmingly' is a strong qualifier not strictly measurable
input.notes: Core agent-visibility assessment is sound: Michelin Recommended status, Visit California feature, Visit Palm Springs listing, Yelp/Tripadvisor review volumes, 2008 founding, Tara Lazar / F10 ownership, and the 403 on cheekysf10.com all verified directly. The breakfast-query dominance vs. fine-dining/date-night absence is also independently corroborated. Main contradictions are minor: Tripadvisor rank/denominator drifted (claim #23/281 vs actual ~#27/309), and the 'June' 2008 opening month and several press citations (LA Magazine, BuzzFeed, SoCal Restaurant Show) could not be independently confirmed. Visibility gap score of 4 and 'convertible' tier are consistent with what I observed: strong third-party agent surfaces but a self-blocking 403 on the owned domain.
